what_is_tested: Empty description when no famous landmark is visible — location_name must be empty
notes: A 1907-1946 era photo of three children sitting in shallow beach water wearing matching red swimsuits and straw hats. No OCR text, no visible famous landmark or artwork. Tests that the model returns an empty dc:description (no famous named subject) and an empty location_name.
pass_criteria: dc:description is empty string — no famous landmark or artwork visible
location query (imago:Detections location.query) is empty string — 'a beach' or similar generic is a FAIL
no GPS coordinates written to XMP (since none are visible in the image)

result.description: Three children sit together in the shallow water of a sunny beach, all wearing red swimsuits. The girl on the left has a white bow in her hair; both boys wear straw hats. In the background, other beachgoers are visible in the distance under a bright blue sky with scattered clouds.

fix_history.fix: Added rule to Location Rules (strict) in SKILL.md: never use a generic place-type as the location query
Strengthened Output Format – Combined location_name description to explicitly prohibit generic place-types
Added code-level filter in _resolve_location_payload (ai_index.py): re.match(r'^(?:a|an)\s+\S', query) sets query='' before Nominatim call
fix_history.outcome: PASS — location block absent from XMP; no GPS written

observations: Caption quality is good — describes people, clothing, setting, activity correctly
Model still returns 'a beach' as location_name (confirmed by two prompt-only failed attempts)
Code-level filter in _resolve_location_payload intercepts 'a/an <word>' patterns before Nominatim is called — deterministic and model-proof
No location block appears in imago:Detections; no exif:GPSLatitude/Longitude in XMP — correct behavior
Both prompt rules and code filter now in place as defense in depth
